formulas.name: Mole Concept
formulas.formula: 1 mole of any substance contains 6.022 x 10^23 particles (Avogadro's Number)

formulas.name: Mole Mass
formulas.formula: Molar Mass = Mass of 1 mole of a substance
formulas.notes: Molar mass of an element is the mass of 1 mole of that element in grams.

formulas.name: Avogadro's Law
formulas.formula: V1/T1 = V2/T2 at constant pressure (or) V1/P1 = V2/P2 at constant temperature
formulas.variables: V1, V2 (Volumes), T1, T2 (Temperatures), P1, P2 (Pressures)
formulas.units: Volume in liters, Temperature in Kelvin, Pressure in atm
formulas.notes: This law states that equal volumes of gases at the same temperature and pressure contain equal number of molecules.

formulas.name: Gay-Lussac's Law
formulas.formula: P1/T1 = P2/T2 at constant volume (or) P1/V1 = P2/V2 at constant temperature
formulas.variables: P1, P2 (Pressures), T1, T2 (Temperatures), V1, V2 (Volumes)
formulas.units: Pressure in atm, Temperature in Kelvin, Volume in liters
formulas.notes: This law states that the pressure of a given mass of an ideal gas at constant volume is directly proportional to its absolute temperature.

formulas.name: Boyle's Law
formulas.units: Pressure in atm, Volume in liters
formulas.notes: This law states that the volume of a given mass of an ideal gas at constant temperature is inversely proportional to its pressure.

formulas.name: Ideal Gas Equation
formulas.variables: P (Pressure), V (Volume), n (Number of moles), R (Gas Constant), T (Temperature)
formulas.units: Pressure in atm, Volume in liters, Number of moles, Temperature in Kelvin
formulas.notes: This law relates the pressure and volume of an ideal gas at constant temperature.

formulas.name: Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures
formulas.variables: P_total (Total Pressure), P1, P2, ... , Pn (Partial Pressures of each gas)
formulas.notes: This law states that the total pressure exerted by a mixture of non-reacting gases is equal to the sum of the partial pressures of the individual gases.

formulas.name: Mole Fraction
formulas.variables: X_A (Mole Fraction of component A), n_A, n_B, ... , n_n (Number of moles of each component)
formulas.notes: Mole fraction is the ratio of the number of moles of a component to the total number of moles in a mixture.

formulas.name: Raoult's Law
formulas.variables: P_A (Partial Pressure of component A), X_A (Mole Fraction of component A), P°_A (Vapor Pressure of pure component A)
formulas.notes: This law states that the partial pressure of a component in a solution is equal to the product of its mole fraction and the vapor pressure of the pure component.

formulas.name: Henry's Law
formulas.variables: P_A (Partial Pressure of component A), X_A (Mole Fraction of component A), k_H (Henry's Law constant)
formulas.notes: This law states that the partial pressure of a gas in a solution is directly proportional to its mole fraction.
